sections.paragraph:
ἀπατηλός, ή, όν (s. ἀπάτη; Hom. et al.; also 4 Macc 18:8 v.l.; Philo; Just., A II, 2, 11; Ath. 27, 2) deceptive ἀνδρὶ ξένῳ ἀπα[τηλοὺς] καὶ ποικίλους καὶ κενοὺς λόγους διδάσκοντι a foreigner who makes deceptive, specious, and nonsensical statements AcPl Ox 6, 11f (=Aa I 241, 14).

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

AcPl Ox 6 AcPl Ox 6 = Acts of Paul, POxy 6—List 1

Aa Aa = Acta apostolorum apocrypha—List 5

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 99
